title: Decision concerning complaint 469/2026/MIK against the European Commission
text: Dear Ms X, You submitted a complaint to the European Ombudsman, on behalf of Corporate Europe Observatory, against the European Commission concerning how it prepared the legislative proposal for a regulation simplifying certain requirements and procedures for chemical products (COM(2025)526, Chemicals Omnibus - Omnibus VI). [1] I would like to thank you for bringing this case to my attention. In your complaint, you argue that the Commission failed to comply with EU primary law and its ‘Better Regulation’ rules. In particular, you point out that the Commission failed to carry out a public consultation in relation to the proposal. You also contend that, while preparing it, the Commission relied largely on information provided by the industry concerned. Moreover, you consider that the Commission acted in urgency without proper justification, and that it proposed measures that will undermine the protection of public health and the environment. My Office has recently received several complaints concerning the Commission’s compliance with its ‘Better Regulation’ rules in preparing legislative proposals that it deemed urgent. The issues raised in these complaints are very similar to those you have brought to my attention. Last year, I opened inquiries into three such complaints, [2] and these inquiries cover, among other issues, the Commission’s failure to adequately justify the urgency of its legislative proposals while derogating from the preparation of these proposals’ full impact assessments, and the Commission’s failure to ensure wide and balanced consultation of its proposals. On 25 November 2025, I found [3] that how the Commission had prepared the legislative proposals at issue in the three complaints constituted maladministration and I issued two recommendations and four suggestions for improvement to the Commission, aimed at addressing the shortcomings I identified. Specifically, I recommended that the Commission should ensure a predictable, consistent and non-arbitrary application of its Better Regulation rules, by defining the notion of ‘urgency’ and providing clear explanations for situations that justify a derogation from the requirements set out in the rules. Furthermore, where derogations are granted, the Commission should establish a procedure to ensure that the urgent preparation of legislative proposals still complies with the principles of a transparent, evidence-based and inclusive law-making process. To assist the Commission in this task, I also made four suggestions for improvement, which include clarifying its stakeholder consultation rules for urgent proposals and ensuring that the evidence supporting its proposals is published in good time to enable a public debate before legislation is adopted. I obtained the Commission’s opinion on my recommendations and suggestions on 18 February 2026. [4] My Office is currently analysing this opinion in detail. You can follow the progress of these inquires on my website, where I have published the Commission’s opinion and where my decision will also be published. [5] While I attach great importance to the issues you have raised in your complaint, I also note that they are largely covered by my above recommendations and suggestions. Therefore, I consider that, at this stage, a separate inquiry into your complaint is not warranted. [6] I have therefore closed the case. That said, I will consider the specific arguments you raise in your complaint in the context of analysing the Commission’s opinion on my recommendations. I have also decided to inform the Commission of your complaint, as it suggests that the issues addressed in my recommendation are not limited to the legislative proposals in question in the three cases I inquired into. As you will also be aware, the Commission has launched a call for evidence on its better regulation framework, inviting interested stakeholders to provide feedback on how its better regulation policy could be further improved to remain fit for purpose and future challenges. While the timeline for responding has passed, you can find more information on the Commission’s ‘ Have Your Say ’ consultation website [7] . I hope you will find these explanations useful. Yours sincerely, Teresa Anjinho European Ombudsman Strasbourg, 09/03/2026 [1] https://single-market-economy.ec.europa.eu/publications/simplification-certain-requirements-and-procedures-chemical-products_en [2] Complaint 983/2025/MAS, complaint 2031/2025/VB and complaint 1379/2024/MIK. [3] Recommendation on the European Commission’s compliance with ‘Better Regulation’ rules and other procedural requirements in preparing legislative proposals that it considered to be urgent (983/2025/MAS - the “Omnibus” case, 2031/2024/VB - the “migration” case, and 1379/2024/MIK - the “CAP” case), https://www.ombudsman.europa.eu/en/recommendation/en/215920 . [4] https://www.ombudsman.europa.eu/en/doc/correspondence/en/220471 [5] https://www.ombudsman.europa.eu/en/case/en/68856 [6] Full information on the procedure and rights pertaining to complaints can be found at https://www.ombudsman.europa.eu/en/document/70707 . [7] https://ec.europa.eu/info/law/better-regulation/have-your-say/initiatives/16232-Communication-on-better-regulation_en
